questions.questionText: In which quadrant does the point (4, 5) lie?
questions.explanation: When both the x-coordinate and y-coordinate are positive, the point is in Quadrant I.

questions.questionText: In which quadrant does the point (-3, 7) lie?
questions.explanation: When the x-coordinate is negative and the y-coordinate is positive, the point is in Quadrant II.

questions.questionText: In which quadrant does the point (-2, -8) lie?
questions.explanation: When both the x-coordinate and y-coordinate are negative, the point is in Quadrant III.

questions.questionText: In which quadrant does the point (6, -1) lie?
questions.explanation: When the x-coordinate is positive and the y-coordinate is negative, the point is in Quadrant IV.

questions.questionText: What is the x-coordinate of the point (-9, 4)?
questions.explanation: In a coordinate pair (x, y), the x-coordinate is the first number. For (-9, 4), the x-coordinate is -9.

questions.questionText: What is the y-coordinate of the point (5, -2)?
questions.explanation: In a coordinate pair (x, y), the y-coordinate is the second number. For (5, -2), the y-coordinate is -2.

questions.questionText: A point lies on the y-axis. Which of the following could be its coordinates?
questions.explanation: Any point on the y-axis must have an x-coordinate of 0. So, (0, 3) is on the y-axis.

questions.questionText: A point lies on the x-axis at x = -5. What are its coordinates?
questions.explanation: Any point on the x-axis must have a y-coordinate of 0. Since x = -5, the coordinates are (-5, 0).

questions.questionText: What are the coordinates of the point (3, -4) after it is reflected in the x-axis?
questions.explanation: To reflect a point in the x-axis, you change the sign of the y-coordinate. So, (3, -4) becomes (3, 4).

questions.questionText: What are the coordinates of the point (-5, 2) after it is reflected in the y-axis?
questions.explanation: To reflect a point in the y-axis, you change the sign of the x-coordinate. So, (-5, 2) becomes (5, 2).

questions.questionText: Find the coordinates of the midpoint of the line segment joining (2, 6) and (8, 10).
Find the coordinates of the midpoint of the line segment joining (-3, 5) and (7, -1).
